summary: Docker-based PoC confirms that server accepts client-controlled X-File-Total-Size claiming 1TB without validation, demonstrating unbounded resource consumption vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: Server should accept chunked upload with claimed 1TB size despite sending only 1KB of actual data, demonstrating lack of size validation.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh showed server accepted the claimed 1TB total size header and created temporary upload file without validation, confirming the DoS vulnerability.
verification_method: verify.sh checks server response for acceptance of upload with claimed size and examines temporary file creation, distinguishing from size validation errors.
evidence_produced_by_run: Temporary file created for upload without size validation
No error returned for claimed size exceeding reasonable limits
Upload proceeded despite minimal actual data (1KB)
Server did not validate claimed size against actual chunk data

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out HEAD, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
